title: frangula rubra subsp. nevadensis
intro: Frangula rubra subsp. nevadensis, also known as Red Buckthorn, is a plant species belonging to the Rhamnaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant's bark has been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and laxative properties due to its high content of anthraquinones. The medicinal properties of Frangula rubra subsp. nevadensis have been found to provide relief from digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, and may also have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
